evidence: The word 'adorable' strongly resonates with aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and semantic meaning (ontological.semantic). It emphasizes individual-scale beauty (scale.individual), essential resonance (resonance.essential), and inclusivity (symmetry.inclusive). Scores are low elsewhere as the term focuses on immediate sensory pleasure and lacks ties to systemic or existential dimensions.

The word 'appeal' as a verb tied to beauty strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10), semantic meaning (ontological.semantic 10), essential resonance (resonance.essential 10), and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic 10). Lower scores reflect its role in triggering responses (interconnection.catalytic 5), personal experience (epistemological.experience 5), and inclusive dynamics (symmetry.inclusive 5).

Lower scores acknowledge structural patterns (ontological.structural:5) and mutual resonance (interconnection.resonant:5) in beauty. Predictable lawfulness (lawfulness.predictable:5) and discovery (experience.discovery:5) highlight expected sensory pleasure and novel encounters with beauty.
